Credit Card Fraud Detection - SQL & R Analytics

Rajvi Kapadia

2026-07-08

transaction <- read_csv(
  "train_transaction.csv",
  col_select = c(TransactionID, TransactionAmt, isFraud, ProductCD,
                 card4, card6, TransactionDT, addr1, addr2, dist1),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

identity <- read_csv(
  "train_identity.csv",
  col_select = c(TransactionID, DeviceType, DeviceInfo),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

SQL

# Create SQLite database and load tables
con <- dbConnect(SQLite(), "fraud_database.sqlite")
dbWriteTable(con, "transactions", transaction, overwrite = TRUE)
dbWriteTable(con, "identity", identity, overwrite = TRUE)
# Query 1: Match transactions to identity records
q1 <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT t.TransactionID, t.TransactionAmt, t.isFraud,
         i.DeviceType, i.DeviceInfo
  FROM transactions t
  LEFT JOIN identity i ON t.TransactionID = i.TransactionID
  LIMIT 10
")
knitr::kable(q1)
TransactionID TransactionAmt isFraud DeviceType DeviceInfo
2987000 68.5 0 NA NA
2987001 29.0 0 NA NA
2987002 59.0 0 NA NA
2987003 50.0 0 NA NA
2987004 50.0 0 mobile SAMSUNG SM-G892A Build/NRD90M
2987005 49.0 0 NA NA
2987006 159.0 0 NA NA
2987007 422.5 0 NA NA
2987008 15.0 0 mobile iOS Device
2987009 117.0 0 NA NA 
# Query 2: Fraud rate by device type
q2 <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT i.DeviceType,
         COUNT(*) AS total_transactions,
         SUM(t.isFraud) AS fraud_count,
         ROUND(SUM(t.isFraud) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) AS fraud_rate_pct
  FROM transactions t
  LEFT JOIN identity i ON t.TransactionID = i.TransactionID
  GROUP BY i.DeviceType
  ORDER BY fraud_rate_pct DESC
")
q2
##   DeviceType total_transactions fraud_count fraud_rate_pct
## 1     mobile              55645        5657          10.17
## 2    desktop              85165        5554           6.52
## 3       <NA>             449730        9452           2.10
# Query 3: Bucket transactions into risk tiers by amount
q3 <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT 
    CASE 
      WHEN TransactionAmt < 50  THEN 'Low (<50)'
      WHEN TransactionAmt < 200 THEN 'Medium (50-200)'
      WHEN TransactionAmt < 500 THEN 'High (200-500)'
      ELSE 'Very High (500+)'
    END AS risk_tier,
    COUNT(*) AS total,
    SUM(isFraud) AS fraud_count,
    ROUND(SUM(isFraud) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) AS fraud_rate_pct
  FROM transactions
  GROUP BY risk_tier
  ORDER BY fraud_rate_pct DESC
")
q3
##          risk_tier  total fraud_count fraud_rate_pct
## 1 Very High (500+)  24324        1149           4.72
## 2   High (200-500)  76146        3417           4.49
## 3        Low (<50) 187515        7480           3.99
## 4  Medium (50-200) 302555        8617           2.85
# Query 4: Flag transactions above the overall average spend
q4 <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT TransactionID, TransactionAmt, isFraud,
         ROUND((SELECT AVG(TransactionAmt) FROM transactions), 2) AS avg_amt,
         CASE WHEN TransactionAmt > (SELECT AVG(TransactionAmt) FROM transactions)
              THEN 'Above Average' ELSE 'Below Average'
         END AS spend_flag
  FROM transactions
  LIMIT 10
")
q4
##    TransactionID TransactionAmt isFraud avg_amt    spend_flag
## 1        2987000           68.5       0  135.03 Below Average
## 2        2987001           29.0       0  135.03 Below Average
## 3        2987002           59.0       0  135.03 Below Average
## 4        2987003           50.0       0  135.03 Below Average
## 5        2987004           50.0       0  135.03 Below Average
## 6        2987005           49.0       0  135.03 Below Average
## 7        2987006          159.0       0  135.03 Above Average
## 8        2987007          422.5       0  135.03 Above Average
## 9        2987008           15.0       0  135.03 Below Average
## 10       2987009          117.0       0  135.03 Below Average
# Query 5: Cumulative fraud count over transaction time
q5 <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT TransactionID, TransactionDT, isFraud,
         SUM(isFraud) OVER (ORDER BY TransactionDT) AS cumulative_fraud_count
  FROM transactions
  ORDER BY TransactionDT
  LIMIT 10
")
q5
##    TransactionID TransactionDT isFraud cumulative_fraud_count
## 1        2987000         86400       0                      0
## 2        2987001         86401       0                      0
## 3        2987002         86469       0                      0
## 4        2987003         86499       0                      0
## 5        2987004         86506       0                      0
## 6        2987005         86510       0                      0
## 7        2987006         86522       0                      0
## 8        2987007         86529       0                      0
## 9        2987008         86535       0                      0
## 10       2987009         86536       0                      0
# Query 6: High-risk device AND high transaction amount combos
q6 <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  WITH high_risk_devices AS (
    SELECT t.TransactionID, t.TransactionAmt, t.isFraud, i.DeviceType
    FROM transactions t
    LEFT JOIN identity i ON t.TransactionID = i.TransactionID
    WHERE i.DeviceType = 'mobile'
  )
  SELECT DeviceType,
         COUNT(*) AS total,
         SUM(isFraud) AS fraud_count,
         ROUND(AVG(TransactionAmt), 2) AS avg_amt,
         ROUND(SUM(isFraud) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) AS fraud_rate_pct
  FROM high_risk_devices
  WHERE TransactionAmt > 200
  GROUP BY DeviceType
")
q6
##   DeviceType total fraud_count avg_amt fraud_rate_pct
## 1     mobile  2404         680  338.15          28.29
# Query 7: Reusable suspicious transactions view
dbExecute(con, "DROP VIEW IF EXISTS suspicious_transactions")
## [1] 0
dbExecute(con, "
  CREATE VIEW suspicious_transactions AS
  SELECT t.TransactionID, t.TransactionAmt, t.isFraud,
         i.DeviceType, i.DeviceInfo
  FROM transactions t
  LEFT JOIN identity i ON t.TransactionID = i.TransactionID
  WHERE t.TransactionAmt > 500 AND t.isFraud = 1
")
## [1] 0
q7 <- dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT * FROM suspicious_transactions LIMIT 10")
q7
##    TransactionID TransactionAmt isFraud DeviceType DeviceInfo
## 1        2987923         774.00       1       <NA>       <NA>
## 2        2988081        2161.00       1       <NA>       <NA>
## 3        2988966         900.00       1       <NA>       <NA>
## 4        2988992         994.00       1       <NA>       <NA>
## 5        2989544         632.00       1       <NA>       <NA>
## 6        2990876         527.00       1       <NA>       <NA>
## 7        2991229        1187.00       1       <NA>       <NA>
## 8        2994663         600.00       1       <NA>       <NA>
## 9        2995267         633.92       1       <NA>       <NA>
## 10       2995311         667.84       1       <NA>       <NA>

Data Preparation & EDA

merged <- transaction %>%
  left_join(identity, by="TransactionID")

dim(merged)
## [1] 590540     12
# High-level summary of key columns
merged %>%
  select(isFraud, TransactionAmt, ProductCD, card4, card6, DeviceType) %>%
  skim()
Data summary
Name Piped data
Number of rows 590540
Number of columns 6
_______________________

numeric 2
________________________

Variable type: character

skim_variable n_missing complete_rate min max empty n_unique whitespace
ProductCD 0 1.00 1 1 0 5 0
card4 1577 1.00 4 16 0 4 0
card6 1571 1.00 5 15 0 4 0
DeviceType 449730 0.24 6 7 0 2 0

Variable type: numeric

skim_variable n_missing complete_rate mean sd p0 p25 p50 p75 p100 hist
isFraud 0 1 0.03 0.18 0.00 0.00 0.00 0 1.00 ▇▁▁▁▁
TransactionAmt 0 1 135.03 239.16 0.25 43.32 68.77 125 31937.39 ▇▁▁▁▁ 
# How imbalanced is the fraud label?
merged %>%
  count(isFraud) %>%
  mutate(pct = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 2))
## # A tibble: 2 × 3
##   isFraud      n   pct
##     <dbl>  <int> <dbl>
## 1       0 569877  96.5
## 2       1  20663   3.5
# Fraud rate by product category
merged %>%
  group_by(ProductCD) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    fraud_count = sum(isFraud),
    fraud_rate = round(fraud_count / total * 100, 2)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(fraud_rate))
## # A tibble: 5 × 4
##   ProductCD  total fraud_count fraud_rate
##   <chr>      <int>       <dbl>      <dbl>
## 1 C          68519        8008      11.7 
## 2 S          11628         686       5.9 
## 3 H          33024        1574       4.77
## 4 R          37699        1426       3.78
## 5 W         439670        8969       2.04
# Fraud rate by card type
merged %>%
  group_by(card4) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    fraud_count = sum(isFraud),
    fraud_rate = round(fraud_count / total * 100, 2)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(fraud_rate))
## # A tibble: 5 × 4
##   card4             total fraud_count fraud_rate
##   <chr>             <int>       <dbl>      <dbl>
## 1 discover           6651         514       7.73
## 2 visa             384767       13373       3.48
## 3 mastercard       189217        6496       3.43
## 4 american express   8328         239       2.87
## 5 <NA>               1577          41       2.6

Feature Engineering

merged <- merged %>%
  mutate(
    # Time-based features
    hour_of_day     = (TransactionDT / 3600) %% 24,
    is_night        = ifelse(hour_of_day >= 22 | hour_of_day <= 6, 1, 0),
    is_morning_peak = ifelse(hour_of_day >= 8 & hour_of_day <= 10, 1, 0),

    # Amount-based features
    amt_log         = log1p(TransactionAmt),
    amt_ratio       = TransactionAmt / median(TransactionAmt, na.rm = TRUE),
    is_high_amount  = ifelse(TransactionAmt > 500, 1, 0),
    is_round_amount = ifelse(TransactionAmt %% 1 == 0, 1, 0),

    # Identity and device features
    no_identity     = ifelse(is.na(DeviceType), 1, 0),
    is_mobile       = ifelse(!is.na(DeviceType) & DeviceType == "mobile", 1, 0),

    # Product risk flag
    is_product_c    = ifelse(ProductCD == "C", 1, 0),

    # Target as factor for plots
    isFraud_factor  = factor(isFraud, levels = c(0, 1), 
                             labels = c("Legit", "Fraud"))
  )

Visualizations

merged %>%
  count(isFraud_factor) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = isFraud_factor, y = n, fill = isFraud_factor)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(n/sum(n)*100, 1), "%")), 
            vjust = -0.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Legit" = "#2196F3", "Fraud" = "#F44336")) +
  labs(title = "Class Imbalance: Fraud vs Legitimate Transactions",
       x = NULL, y = "Transaction Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

merged %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = amt_log, fill = isFraud_factor)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Legit" = "#2196F3", "Fraud" = "#F44336")) +
  labs(title = "Transaction Amount Distribution (Log Scale)",
       x = "Log Transaction Amount", y = "Density", fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal()

merged %>%
  group_by(ProductCD) %>%
  summarise(fraud_rate = round(mean(isFraud) * 100, 2)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(ProductCD, fraud_rate), y = fraud_rate, 
             fill = fraud_rate)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(fraud_rate, "%")), 
            hjust = -0.2, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#90CAF9", high = "#F44336") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Fraud Rate by Product Category",
       x = "Product Code", y = "Fraud Rate (%)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

merged %>%
  group_by(hour = round(hour_of_day)) %>%
  summarise(fraud_rate = mean(isFraud) * 100) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = hour, y = fraud_rate)) +
  geom_line(color = "#F44336", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(color = "#F44336", size = 2) +
  labs(title = "Fraud Rate by Hour of Day",
       x = "Hour (0 = Midnight)", y = "Fraud Rate (%)") +
  theme_minimal()

merged %>%
  mutate(device_label = case_when(
    is.na(DeviceType)       ~ "No Identity Record",
    DeviceType == "mobile"  ~ "Mobile",
    DeviceType == "desktop" ~ "Desktop",
    TRUE                    ~ "Other"
  )) %>%
  group_by(device_label) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    fraud_rate = round(mean(isFraud) * 100, 2)
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(device_label, fraud_rate), y = fraud_rate, 
             fill = fraud_rate)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(fraud_rate, "%")), 
            hjust = -0.2, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#90CAF9", high = "#F44336") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Fraud Rate by Device Type",
       x = NULL, y = "Fraud Rate (%)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

Logistic Regression Model

model_data <- merged %>%
  select(isFraud, amt_log, amt_ratio, is_round_amount,
         is_night, is_morning_peak, is_high_amount,
         no_identity, is_mobile, is_product_c) %>%
  drop_na()

nrow(model_data)
## [1] 590540
# Train/test split: 80% train, 20% test
set.seed(42)

fraud_rows <- which(model_data$isFraud == 1)
normal_rows <- which(model_data$isFraud == 0)


train_idx <- c(
  sample(fraud_rows, floor(length(fraud_rows)*0.8)),
  sample(normal_rows, floor(length(normal_rows)*0.8))
)


train_data <- model_data[train_idx, ]
test_data  <- model_data[-train_idx, ]

# Fit logistic regression
model <- glm(isFraud ~ ., data = train_data, family = binomial)
summary(model)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = isFraud ~ ., family = binomial, data = train_data)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                  Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)     -6.395882   0.064098 -99.783  < 2e-16 ***
## amt_log          0.398789   0.012228  32.614  < 2e-16 ***
## amt_ratio        0.008861   0.003267   2.712  0.00668 ** 
## is_round_amount  1.382020   0.029893  46.232  < 2e-16 ***
## is_night         0.038610   0.016828   2.294  0.02177 *  
## is_morning_peak  1.080681   0.058350  18.521  < 2e-16 ***
## is_high_amount  -0.082095   0.053679  -1.529  0.12617    
## no_identity     -0.329494   0.023305 -14.138  < 2e-16 ***
## is_mobile        0.424165   0.022636  18.739  < 2e-16 ***
## is_product_c     2.746538   0.037033  74.164  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 143316  on 472430  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 128174  on 472421  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 128194
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 7
# Predict probabilities on test set
test_data$predicted_prob <- predict(model, newdata = test_data, 
                                    type = "response")

# ROC curve and AUC
roc_obj <- roc(test_data$isFraud, test_data$predicted_prob)
auc_val <- round(auc(roc_obj), 4)
cat("AUC:", auc_val, "\n")
## AUC: 0.7562
# Plot ROC curve
ggroc(roc_obj, color = "#F44336", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 1, xend = 0, y = 0, yend = 1),
               linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.4, y = 0.2,
           label = paste("AUC =", auc_val),
           size = 5, fontface = "bold", color = "#F44336") +
  labs(title = "ROC Curve — Logistic Regression Fraud Model",
       x = "1 - Specificity", y = "Sensitivity") +
  theme_minimal()

coef_df <- data.frame(
  term = names(coef(model))[-1],
  estimate = exp(coef(model)[-1])
)


ggplot(coef_df,
       aes(
         x = reorder(term, estimate),
         y = estimate
       )) +
  geom_point(size=3) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 1,
    linetype="dashed"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title="Odds Ratios — Logistic Regression",
    x=NULL,
    y="Odds Ratio"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

dbDisconnect(con)
